articles.title: Shai Hulud attack ships signed malicious TanStack, Mistral npm packages
articles.summary: Hundreds of packages across npm and PyPI have been compromised in a new Shai-Hulud supply-chain campaign delivering credential-stealing malware targeting developers. [...]

articles.title: Claude Mythos Finds Only One Curl Vulnerability; Experts Divided on What It Really Means
articles.summary: Curl’s lead developer says Mythos claims are marketing, but many in the industry believe the results stem from Curl’s robust security. The post Claude Mythos Finds Only One Curl Vulnerability; Experts Divided on What It Really Means appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Copy.Fail Linux Vulnerability
articles.summary: This is the worst Linux vulnerability in years. TL;DR copy.fail is a Linux kernel local privilege escalation, not a browser or clipboard attack. articles.title: TanStack, Mistral AI, UiPath Hit in Fresh Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: Over 400 malicious versions of 170 packages were published as part of the new Mini Shai-Hulud campaign. The post TanStack, Mistral AI, UiPath Hit in Fresh Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Instructure reaches 'agreement' with ShinyHunters to stop data leak
articles.summary: Instructure, the edtech giant behind the widely popular Canvas learning management system (LMS), has reached an "agreement" with the ShinyHunters extortion group to prevent the data stolen in a recent breach from being leaked online. [...]

articles.title: New GhostLock tool abuses Windows API to block file access

articles.title: Build Application Firewalls Aim to Stop the Next Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: Rather than scanning code alone, Build Application Firewalls inspect runtime behavior inside the software build pipeline. The post Build Application Firewalls Aim to Stop the Next Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Why Changing Passwords Doesn’t End an Active Directory Breach
articles.summary: Resetting a password doesn't always remove attackers from Active Directory. Specops Software explains how cached credentials and Kerberos tickets can keep attackers authenticated after a reset. [...]

articles.summary: The zero-day was designed to bypass 2FA and it was developed by a prominent cybercrime group. The post Google Detects First AI-Generated Zero-Day Exploit appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: UK water company allowed hackers to lurk undetected for nearly two years, regulator finds
articles.summary: The Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) fined South Staffordshire Water £963,900 ($1.3 million) on Monday over an attack by the Cl0p ransomware group that led to the personal data of 633,887 customers and employees being published in August 2022.

articles.summary: This upcoming webinar explores how organizations need to combine security, backups, and recovery planning to reduce the impact of modern cyberattacks. [...]

articles.title: Dirty Frag: Linux kernel hit by second major security flaw in two weeks
articles.summary: The issue was found in the same area of the Linux kernel that produced last month’s Copy Fail bug, and also allows anyone with a basic account on an affected computer to seize full administrative control.

articles.title: Skoda Data Breach Hits Online Shop Customers
articles.summary: Using a vulnerability in the portal, hackers accessed names, addresses, email addresses, and phone numbers. The post Skoda Data Breach Hits Online Shop Customers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: Defending a network at 2 am looks a lot like this: an analyst copy-pasting a hash from a PDF into a SIEM query. articles.title: SailPoint Discloses GitHub Repository Hack
articles.summary: The incident occurred on April 20 and did not affect customer data in the company’s production and staging environments. The post SailPoint Discloses GitHub Repository Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Checkmarx Jenkins AST Plugin Compromised in Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: A malicious version of the plugin was published to the Jenkins Marketplace late last week. The post Checkmarx Jenkins AST Plugin Compromised in Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: A new variant of the TrickMo Android banking malware, delivered in campaigns targeting users across Europe, introduces new commands and uses The Open Network (TON) for stealthy command-and-control communications. [...]

articles.title: Canvas System Is Online After a Cyberattack Disrupted Thousands of Schools

The post Canvas System Is Online After a Cyberattack Disrupted Thousands of Schools appeared first on SecurityWeek.
